content:
3 min read
Just now

--

My academic day is complete only when I open the Goodnotes app. That’s where my understandings take shape.
Goodnotes limited is a private software company headquartered in London. The app is globally popular AI-powered digital note-taking application.
This app is so convenient that you may get addicted to notes making.
So, what is that feature making it so addictive ?
These are the features, which were introduced into the application in the long run with thorough data analysis from the data collected from the users through multiple mode. It is not just a showcase of the technical know-how but also a design to benefit the customers.
There are plenty of note making tools. But this particular app has the right combination of required tools for seamless note-making from clear design thinking and the problem solving process. The tools such as pen, lasso, shape, images, stickers, book outliner are making it easy. Also this app holds all at once – text, image, drawing, annotation, highlighter.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

How is this app so precise in addressing the problem ?
It is based on the data that it is collected over the period of time from the users.
Anonymous statistical or user generated data (crash frequency, feature usage, user feedback) to enhance the app experience and to introduce new tools.
Data & it’s use :

• Handwriting data : To create an individual digital template or profile for the purpose of identifying the user. It also makes it easy to convert the writings to text.

• “Export diagnostics data” feature : Tries to prevent any potential disruptions or Cyber-attacks. App performance can be assessed when large number of people are using the app.

• The particular page that is visited in any of their sites

• Carrying out Analytics from queries and prompts from users : Provide and improve the Goodnotes AI features by training thier AI models

• To obtain feedback on the Services via surveys and similar methods, such as interactive features.

• To monitor use of: (i) digital products (including planners, notebooks and stickers); and (ii) digital textbooks through digital rights management solutions.
These are some examples on how a data is collected and used which is picked up from Goodnotes website in Goodnotes privacy policy.
The data is a powerful tool which is helping the company as well as the user. User is getting benefits and the company is understanding the customer value. Without the data, analysing people from around the world would be possible, but the app would never have come into effect. So, such huge data is not just helping us find the customer value, but also speeds up the decision making process and stays up-to-date.
Note: The “Data and its use” content in my blog was summarised by me from Goodnotes privacy policy.
